data.content: Now I'll create the template configuration. The `.template.config/template.json` file uses `sourceName` to auto-replace `MyTests` with whatever name the user provides via `dotnet new`.
data.reasoningText:
Now I need to create a .NET template from this project. A .NET template requires a `.template.config` directory with a `template.json` file inside the project directory. I'll also need to make the source files use template parameters so the namespace and class names are replaced with the user's project name.

Let me create:
1. `.template.config/template.json` - the template configuration
2. Update the source files to use template parameters (sourceName replacement)

The `sourceName` mechanism in .NET templates will automatically replace occurrences of the source name with the user's chosen project name. I'll set `sourceName` to `MyTests` so it gets replaced throughout.
